meta.title: Plasma-Catalytic Ammonia Synthesis Beyond the Equilibrium Limit
meta.notes: Figure-read values for Figs 3,4,5 are caption/text-derived; plotted point coordinates not transcribed individually. Char spans left null.

claims.text: The ammonia synthesis reaction is exergonic at near-ambient conditions, with ΔG°(298 K) = −32.8 kJ mol−1.

location.quote:
This reaction is exergonic at near-ambient conditions
(∆G◦(298 K) = −32.8 kJ mol−1)

claims.predicate: reported_delta_G_298K_kJ_per_mol == -32.8

claims.text: Typical industrial-scale NH3 synthesis operates at 700–800 K and 100–200 atm.

location.quote:
synthesis processes thus operate at temperatures in the range of 700–800 K and pressures of
100–200 atm

claims.predicate: reported_industrial_T_range_K == [700,800] AND reported_industrial_P_range_atm == [100,200]

claims.text: Plasma-assisted ammonia synthesis can be performed at milder conditions of ~473 K and 1 atm.

location.quote:
ammonia synthesis can be performed at milder temperatures and pressures (∼473 K, 1 atm) when catalysts are operated alongside non-thermal plas-
mas

claims.predicate: reported_plasma_T_K == 473 AND reported_plasma_P_atm == 1

claims.text: NH3 yields are predicted to exceed bulk thermodynamic equilibrium limits on materials that are thermal-rate-limited by N2 dissociation.

location.quote:
NH3 yields are predicted
to exceed bulk thermodynamic equilibrium limits on materials that are thermal-rate-limited by N2
dissociation.

claims.predicate: model_predicts NH3_yield > bulk_equilibrium_yield for N2-dissociation-limited materials

claims.text: In all cases, yields revert to bulk equilibrium at temperatures at which thermal reaction rates exceed plasma-activated ones.

location.quote:
In all cases, yields revert to bulk equilibrium at temperatures at which thermal
reaction rates exceed plasma-activated ones.

claims.predicate: yields revert to equilibrium when r_thermal > r_plasma

claims.text: Beyond-equilibrium NH3 yields are observed experimentally in a packed bed DBD reactor and depend on catalyst material consistent with model predictions.

location.quote:
Beyond-equilibrium NH3 yields are observed in a
packed bed dielectric-barrier-discharge reactor and exhibit sensitivity to catalytic material choice
in a way consistent with model predictions.

claims.predicate: observed NH3_yield > bulk_equilibrium_yield in DBD reactor; material-dependent consistent with model

claims.text: The CSTR model operates at atmospheric pressure, constant volume of 1 mL, stoichiometric feed at a flow rate of 10 mL min−1, with catalyst site density of 10−3 mol mL−1.

location.quote:
continuously stirred tank reactor (CSTR) operating at atmospheric pressure and constant
volume (1 mL). The reactants are introduced into the reactor in their stoichiometric ratio,
at a flow rate of 10 mL min−1.

claims.predicate: model_volume_mL == 1 AND model_flow_mL_per_min == 10 AND site_density_mol_per_mL == 1e-3

claims.text: The intermediate N-binding catalyst (EN = −0.6 eV) sits at the top of the volcano curve and gives the highest NH3 yields of the three modeled catalysts.

location.quote:
The catalyst that binds
N with intermediate strength (EN = −0.6 eV), sits at the top of the volcano curve (see inset)

claims.predicate: EN_intermediate_eV == -0.6 AND yields highest among three modeled catalysts (Fig 2)

claims.text: Three catalysts are modeled, characterized by strong (EN = −1.2 eV), intermediate (EN = −0.6 eV), and weak (EN = 0.0 eV) nitrogen binding energies; the volcano inset is calculated at 473 K, 1 atm, and 5% conversion of N2.

location.quote:
Three catalysts are shown, characterized by strong (EN = −1.2 eV),
intermediate (EN = −0.6 eV), and weak (EN = 0.0 eV) nitrogen binding energies.

claims.predicate: EN_values_eV == [-1.2,-0.6,0.0] AND inset_conditions == {T_K:473, P_atm:1, conv_pct:5}

claims.text: Upon plasma excitation the N2 dissociation barrier is assumed reduced by 1 eV, corresponding to roughly three N2 vibrational quanta.

location.quote:
we assume that upon excitation to this state, the N2 dissociation
barrier is reduced by 1 eV, corresponding to roughly three N2 vibrational quanta.

claims.predicate: assumed_barrier_reduction_eV == 1 AND ~3 vibrational quanta

claims.text: Plasma-on models predict enhancements in NH3 over thermal catalysis for the EN=−0.6 eV and EN=0.0 eV materials if kene ≥ 10−2 s−1.

location.quote:
the
plasma-on models predict enhancements in NH3 produced over thermal catalysis for both
materials if the rate of plasma-activation of N2 is sufficiently high (kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
).

claims.predicate: enhancement predicted for materials when kene >= 1e-2 s^-1

claims.text: For the optimal thermal catalyst (EN=−0.6 eV), NH3 yields exceeding equilibrium are observed at kene = 10−1 s−1 in the temperature window 475–600 K.

location.quote:
NH3 yields
exceeding equilibrium for the optimal thermal catalyst are observed at kene = 10−1s
−1
, in
the temperature window of 475–600 K.

claims.predicate: beyond_eq for EN=-0.6 at kene==1e-1 s^-1 over T_K in [475,600]

claims.text: For the weaker-binding material, NH3 pressures are predicted to exceed the equilibrium limit for kene ≥ 10−2 s−1.

location.quote:
NH3 pressures are
predicted to go beyond the equilibrium limit for kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
.

claims.predicate: beyond_eq for weaker-binding material when kene >= 1e-2 s^-1

claims.text: For the strong N-binding catalyst (EN=−1.2 eV) no enhancement in NH3 during plasma operation occurs; the surface remains covered with N up to ~600 K.

location.quote:
the surface remains cov-
ered with N up to temperatures as high as 600 K

claims.predicate: no plasma enhancement for EN=-1.2; N coverage persists up to ~600 K

claims.text: In the experiments N2 and H2 were introduced in a 2:1 ratio at a total flow rate of 40 SCCM, and bulk reactor temperature was varied between 423–1123 K.

location.quote:
We
introduced N2 and H2 into the reactor in a 2:1 ratio at a total flow rate of 40 SCCM.

claims.predicate: N2:H2 == 2:1 AND total_flow_SCCM == 40 AND T_range_K == [423,1123]

claims.text: Background experiments were run at plasma powers of 5, 10, and 15 W, corresponding to specific energy inputs of 7.5, 15, and 22.5 kJ L−1.

location.quote:
Reaction performance was evaluated at plasma powers of 5, 10, and 15W,
corresponding to specific energy inputs of 7.5, 15, and 22.5 kJ L−1.

claims.predicate: powers_W == [5,10,15] AND SEI_kJ_per_L == [7.5,15,22.5] consistent with flow 40 SCCM

claims.text: In background experiments NH3 yields increased with plasma power and reactor temperature, exceeding the equilibrium limit, and maximized at about 920 K.

location.quote:
At all plasma powers, NH3 yields maximized at about 920 K, after which they
decreased.

claims.predicate: max NH3 yield occurs at ~920 K (Fig 4); yields increase with power

claims.text: Ni and Pt catalysts both bind N more weakly than the optimal thermal catalyst Ru, with the N-metal bond strength order Ru > Ni > Pt.

location.quote: the N-metal bond strength follows the order Ru > Ni > Pt

claims.predicate: N_metal_bond_strength_order == Ru > Ni > Pt

claims.text: On Pt catalyst, plasma-catalytic NH3 yields exceeded the bulk equilibrium limit and continued to enhance over background until about 1000 K, while on Ni yields fell below background and collapsed onto the equilibrium curve.

location.quote:
Pt continued to enhance NH3 production over the background
at higher temperatures until about 1000 K, after which the yields converged.

claims.predicate: Pt NH3_yield > equilibrium up to ~1000 K; Ni yields collapse to equilibrium (Fig 5a)

claims.text: NH3 decomposition experiments used a feed of 0.5% NH3, 9.5% He, balance 2:1 N2:H2, at 40 mL min−1; Ni/Al2O3 lit off at ~750 K, while Pt/Al2O3 and bare γ-Al2O3 lit off at ~900 K.

location.quote:
Of the tested materials, Ni/Al2O3 is the most active for NH3
decomposition, lighting off at around 750 K. The decomposition curves for Pt/γ-Al2O3 and
metal-free γ-Al2O3 are nearly identical, lighting off at approximately 900 K.

claims.predicate: Ni lightoff_T_K ~750; Pt and bare Al2O3 lightoff_T_K ~900 (Fig 5b)

claims.text: The ammonia synthesis reaction was modeled with ΔH(298 K) = −91.8 kJ/mol.

location.quote: The ammonia synthesis reaction was modeled with ∆H (298 K) = −91.8 kJ/mol.

claims.predicate: modeled_delta_H_298K_kJ_per_mol == -91.8

claims.text: The Boltzmann equation was solved for a N2 plasma at a reduced electric field of 100 Td; ke ranges 10−14–10−9 cm3 s−1 and ne is typically 1014–1015 cm−3.

location.quote:
we solved the Boltzmann equation for a N2 plasma at a reduced electric field of 100 Td [60] using the online solver at
https://us.lxcat.net/. ke typically ranges between 10−14–10−9cm3s
−1 depending on

claims.predicate: reduced_E_field_Td == 100 AND ke_cm3_per_s in [1e-14,1e-9] AND ne_cm-3 in [1e14,1e15]

claims.text: The plasma reactor has a quartz tube 5 mm internal diameter, mesh covering 6 cm, discharge gap 1.75 mm, and discharge volume 1.07 cm3.

location.quote:
the discharge gap
is 1.75 mm, and the discharge volume is 1.07 cm3.

claims.predicate: discharge_gap_mm == 1.75 AND discharge_volume_cm3 == 1.07 AND tube_id_mm == 5 AND mesh_length_cm == 6

claims.text: Catalysts were Pt and Ni on γ-Al2O3 at 5 wt% metal, 100 mg per experiment; particle sizes based on at least 300 particles.

location.quote: Pt and Ni supported on γ-Al2O3 (5 wt%) were obtained from Riogen Inc.

claims.predicate: metal_loading_wt_pct == 5 AND material_mass_mg == 100 AND particles_counted >= 300

claims.text: Each data point is an average of three GC injections with error bars equal to the standard deviation of these three measurements; catalysts pretreated at 773 K for 30 min in 20 SCCM H2.

location.quote:
Each data point is based off
of an average of three injections by the GC and the error bars shown correspond to the
standard deviation of these three measurements.

claims.predicate: n_injections_per_point == 3 AND error_bars == std_dev
